message: I arrived at the St. Regis Hotel, the accommodation for Meryl as told by Director Fabar.
I had heard that Meryl's room was a suite on the top floor, so I was going to head up directly when I ran into a familiar face.
While I was wondering whether to say hello, he came up to me with a woman accompanying him.
Oh, I thought I recognized that face; you’re the ace detective.
He seemed a bit flustered and looked at me in surprise. Apparently, he hadn’t noticed me and just walked over.
Oh, do you know each other?
Just a casual acquaintance, Ginny. We just happened to be at Director Fabar's party together.
So that’s the kind of acquaintance.
The St. Regis Hotel has been used for movie shoots many times in the past.
Its heavy air of history and luxuriousness, fitting for a high-end hotel, probably explains why it’s been chosen for numerous film locations.
Standing in front of me, Tony Ford and his companion Ginny are both eye-catching types, attracting people's attention.
What am I doing here, being the supporting role?
They are looking at me with a sullen expression.
It seems they don’t like that we ran into each other here.
Contrary to their relaxed attitude during the party, they are giving me a reproachful glance.
Maybe we met at a bad time.
Tony’s companion Ginny is a stunningly beautiful woman, smiling as she compares me with Tony.
What is she finding funny while comparing me to Tony?
I don’t recall, but she might be a stage actress? Is she Tony’s girlfriend?
Even though the lobby is heated, isn't she cold dressed like that in this winter weather?
I didn’t expect to run into you in a place like this. Sorry, but I’m in a hurry, so excuse me.
Ah, please wait a moment, Mr. Ford!
If you have something to say, could you make it quick?
Oh, you could talk a little longer!
I don’t have time for that. We need to leave soon or we’ll be late for the concert.
Oh, oh my, that’s really serious!
Well, I’ll take my leave now, Detective-kun.
Saying that, Tonny left with the beautiful woman accompanying him.
Hello, Detective! I’m Ginny, nice to meet you.
Nice to meet you, I'm Jake Snipes.
Ginny-san, your name is lovely too.
Oh, that’s sweet. But just Ginny is fine. When you add “san”, it makes me feel all shy.
Meeting in Boston and reuniting in New York, the world is a small place.
Oh, are you a friend of Guy?
I know Guy really well, you know.
I wanted to go to the party too.
Do you know where Guy's traveling to?
What are you talking about? There's no way I would know something like that.
I thought you might know something since you seem close with Guy.
You're his best friend, right? Why would I know something you don't? Please stop joking.
Can you knock it off already?
I'm in a hurry right now. So please don’t bother me.
You probably don't care about my companion anyway.
It's about the murder case, but do you really think she's the culprit?
Oh, are you saying Tony figured out who the culprit is?
Ginny, don’t trust the talk of a crazy man.
I don't have time to mess around with you.
I'm actually really close with Guy.
Ginny! You shouldn’t say unnecessary things.
Oh, but that’s the truth. What's wrong with speaking the truth?
Actually, there are a lot of things I want to know about Guy.
You're going to ask a girl you just met about your best friend?
Oh, that's not weird at all. Right, Jake?
I think I could tell Jake things about Guy he doesn’t know.
Hey, Ginny! Don't say reckless things; come on, we’re leaving.
I think I can help you.
Call me at this number here. I’ll help you with your questions about Guy. Bye!
Ginny said just that and left the hotel with Tonny.
Hey, what do you think my relationship with Tonny is?
I like guys like Tonny, but I also like you.
Why don’t you cut it out?
Tonny, come on. Are you feeling a bit jealous?
If we ever get the chance to meet alone, I’ll take my time telling you about me.
Talking about it in front of him is a bit...
I'll tell you when we're alone.
Ginny, are you feeling dissatisfied with me?
Come on, don’t sulk in public.
If we meet somewhere else, I'll tell you then.
I visited Meryl's room as instructed by the director.
I visited Meryl's room as instructed by the director.
But there was no response when I knocked on the door.
It seems Meryl is out. Maybe she went to the location shooting site?
